# Coder Prevention Rules (Lessons Learned)

This file is **append-only**. The code-reviewer agent appends dated rules after analyzing `wf:error` tasks.
The coder agent reads this file at the start of every `/implement` cycle to avoid repeating past mistakes.

**Do NOT edit or delete existing entries.** Only append new entries below.

---

<!-- ENTRIES START — append new rules below this line -->

## 2026-06-29 — ESLint does not typecheck; always run pnpm build before completing (related task: 244482c6)
`pnpm lint` runs ESLint only — it does NOT invoke `tsc` and will NOT catch TypeScript type errors. `next build` runs the full TypeScript compiler as part of its pipeline and WILL catch them. A type error that passes `pnpm lint` will still break the CI release build (`beforeBuildCommand: pnpm build` in tauri.conf.json). Always run `pnpm build` (zero errors) in addition to `pnpm lint` before marking any task done. Evidence: `roleI18nKey` returning `string` instead of `TranslationKey` passed lint but broke the v3.0.2 release build (run 28349689538).

## 2026-06-29 — Rust changes need a local cargo build + clippy; never borrow a value after a params! move (error task: 58d56279)
Any task that edits `src-tauri/` MUST be validated locally with `cargo build --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml` AND `cargo clippy --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- -D warnings` before completion — the frontend `pnpm build`/`pnpm lint` gates do not compile Rust, so Rust errors slip straight into the CI release build. Two specific patterns that broke v3.0.4 / v3.0.5: (1) `params![x]` consumes `x` via libsql `into_value` (takes ownership of `self`); if you also need `x` afterward (e.g. `fetch_restaurant_name(&pool, &restaurant_id)` after `params![restaurant_id]`), pass `params![x.clone()]` or use `&x` only after all consuming calls — otherwise `error[E0382]: borrow of moved value`. (2) The CI clippy gate runs with `-D warnings`, so lints like `collapsible_if` are hard errors: write `if a && let Ok(v) = expr {}` (edition-2024 let-chain) instead of a nested `if a { if let Ok(v) = expr {} }`. Both must compile cleanly on the actual target before tagging a release.

## 2026-06-29 — CI error reporter must capture the failing step's log, not just the frontend build (error task: 6554a6f1)
`.github/scripts/report-ci-failure.py` reads only `$RUNNER_TEMP/next-build.log` (the captured `pnpm build` output). The release actually fails later — in the `Rust lint (clippy -D warnings)` step or inside `tauri-apps/tauri-action` (Rust compile/signing) — and that output is never written to `next-build.log`. As a result every `[ci-error]` task shows a *successful* frontend build and omits the real error, which is highly misleading to the reviewer. Fix the workflow so the clippy step and the tauri-action step also `tee` their combined stdout/stderr to a log file (e.g. `$RUNNER_TEMP/rust-build.log`), and have the reporter scan all captured logs (preferring the most recent non-empty one) for error markers including `error[E`, `error:`, `could not compile`, and `##[error]`.

## 2026-07-01 — Never invoke a Tauri command with an optional/undefined required arg; Tauri drops undefined keys (error task: 2d6e99fc)
A Rust `#[tauri::command]` with a non-optional parameter (e.g. `get_exchange_rate(restaurant_id: String, ...)`) REQUIRES that key in the JS args. The frontend `call('get_exchange_rate', { restaurantId: restaurant_id })` serializes `{ restaurantId: undefined }` by dropping the `undefined` key entirely, so Rust rejects it with `invalid args 'restaurantId' ... missing required key restaurantId`. This fired 17× because several callers used the anti-pattern `getExchangeRate(restaurantId || undefined)` (OrderProvider, history, analytics, ExchangeRateManagement), passing `undefined` before a restaurant was selected. Rule: (1) type the API wrapper's arg as REQUIRED (`restaurant_id: string`, not `restaurant_id?: string`) so TS forces callers to supply it; (2) callers must GUARD — early-return / skip the call when `restaurant_id` is falsy rather than coercing to `undefined`; OR (3) if the command legitimately has no tenant context, change the Rust signature to `Option<String>` AND handle `None`. Do not paper over it with a `catch {}` that hides the invoke rejection — the error still reaches the global error relay.

## 2026-07-01 — Refactors must leave no dangling symbol references (missing imports / deleted components) (error task: 969d0d8e)
Two `Uncaught ReferenceError` classes appeared during the sidebar refactor: `ChevronUp is not defined` (an icon used in `SidebarNav.tsx` JSX without being added to the `lucide-react` import) and `MySettingsModal is not defined` (the component file was deleted but JSX still referenced it). Both slip past `pnpm lint` intermittently under HMR but crash the running app and trip the error relay. Rule: whenever you (a) use a new `lucide-react` icon, add it to the import list in the SAME edit; (b) delete or rename a component, grep the whole `src/` tree for the old symbol and remove every reference before completing. Always run `pnpm build` (full `tsc`) after a refactor — an unresolved identifier is a compile error `tsc` catches even though ESLint may not.
